description: United States Army post outside Columbus, Georgia, previously known as Fort Moore
description_wiki: Fort Benning (formerly Fort Moore from 2023–25) is a United States Army post in the Columbus, Georgia, area.

description: city in and county seat of Washoe County, Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Reno (, REE-noh) is a city in the northwest section of the U.S. state of Nevada, along the Nevada–California border.

description: United States Army post
description_wiki: Fort McClellan, originally Camp McClellan, is  a United States Army post located adjacent to the city of Anniston, Alabama.

description: part of the United Kingdom situated on the island of Ireland
description_wiki: Northern Ireland is a part of the United Kingdom in the north-east of the island of Ireland.

description: German submarine of the First or Second World War
description_wiki: U-boats are naval submarines operated by Germany, especially during World War I and World War II. The term is an anglicized form of the German word U-Boot [ˈuːboːt] , a shortening of Unterseeboot (lit. 'under-sea boat').

description: US Army Infantry regiment
description_wiki: The 18th Infantry Regiment ("Vanguards") is an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

description: active United States Army formation, oldest continuously serving division in the Regular Army
description_wiki: The 1st Infantry Division (1ID) is a combined arms division of the United States Army, and is the oldest continuously serving division in the Regular Army.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: US
description_wiki: The 16th Infantry Regiment ("Semper Paratus") is a regiment in the United States Army and has traditionally been a part of the 1st Infantry Division.

description: commune in Manche, France
description_wiki: Saint-Lô (US: , French: [sɛ̃ lo] ; Breton: Sant Lo) is a commune in northwest France, the capital of the Manche department in the region of Normandy.

description: recipient of the Purple Heart medal (1883-1944)
description_wiki: Lesley James McNair (25 May 1883 – 25 July 1944) was an American Army officer who served during World War I and World War II. He attained the rank of lieutenant general during his life; he was killed in action during World War II, and received a posthumous promotion to general.

description: nonprofit-charitable corporation that provides live entertainment, social facilities, and other programs to members of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United Service Organizations Inc.

description: city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Aachen is the 13th-largest city in North Rhine-Westphalia and the 27th-largest city of Germany, with around 261,000 inhabitants.

description: 1944 series of battles fought between US and German forces during World War II in the Hürtgen Forest of Germany
description_wiki: The Battle of Hürtgen Forest (German: Schlacht im Hürtgenwald) was a series of battles fought from 19 September to 16 December 1944, between American and German forces on the Western Front during World War II, in the Hürtgen Forest, a 140 km2 (54 mi2) area about 5 km (3.1 mi) east of the Belgian–German border.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: American submachine gun
description_wiki: The Thompson submachine gun (also known as the "Tommy gun", "Chicago typewriter", or "trench broom") is a blowback-operated, selective-fire submachine gun, invented and developed by Brigadier General John T. Thompson, a United States Army officer, in 1918.

description: semi-automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M1 Carbine (formally the United States Carbine, Caliber .30, M1) is a lightweight semi-automatic carbine chambered in the .30 carbine (7.62×33mm) cartridge that was issued to the U.S. military during World War II, the Korean War, and the Vietnam War.

description: semi-automatic pistol of German origin
semi-automatic pistol
description_wiki: The Walther P38 (originally written Walther P.38) is a 9 mm semi-automatic pistol that was developed by Carl Walther GmbH as the service pistol of the Wehrmacht at the beginning of World War II. It was intended to replace the comparatively complex and expensive to produce Luger P08.

description: town in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
description_wiki: Remagen (German pronunciation: [ˈʁeːmaːɡn̩] ) is a town in Germany in the state of Rhineland-Palatinate, in the district of Ahrweiler.

description: rocket artillery
description_wiki: The Nebelwerfer (transl. "fog launcher") was a World War II German series of weapons.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: public holiday commemorating the surrender of Nazi Germany (8 May 1945)
description_wiki: Victory in Europe Day is the day celebrating the formal acceptance by the Allies of World War II of Nazi Germany's unconditional surrender of its armed forces on Tuesday, 8 May 1945; it marked the official surrender of all German military operations.

description: country in Central Europe, 1918–1992
description_wiki: Czechoslovakia (  CHEK-oh-sloh-VAK-ee-ə, CHEK-ə-, -⁠slə-, -⁠VAH-; Czech and Slovak: Československo, Česko-Slovensko) was a country in Central Europe created in 1918, as Czecho-Slovakia (until 1920), when it declared its independence from Austria-Hungary.

description: constituent republic of the Soviet Union (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic (Russian SFSR) was a communist state from 1917 to 1922, and afterwards the largest and most populous constituent republic of the Soviet Union (USSR) from 1922 to 1991, until becoming a sovereign part of the Soviet Union with priority of Russian laws over Union-level legislation in 1990 and 1991, the last two years of the existence of the USSR. The Russian SFSR was composed of sixteen smaller constituent units of autonomous republics, five autonomous oblasts, ten autonomous okrugs, six krais and forty oblasts.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: municipality in the province of Antwerp, Belgium
description_wiki: Antwerp ( ; Dutch: Antwerpen [ˈɑntʋɛrpə(n)] ; French: Anvers [ɑ̃vɛʁs] ) is a city and a municipality in the Flemish Region of Belgium.

description: class of cargo ship
description_wiki: The Victory ship is a class of cargo ships produced in large numbers by American shipyards during World War II. They were a more modern design compared to the earlier Liberty ship, were slightly larger and had more powerful steam turbine engines, giving them higher speed to allow participation in high-speed convoys and make them more difficult targets for German U-boats.

entity: University of California, Hastings College of the Law
description: bookshop in San Francisco, CA, United States of America
